name: Urrutia for NH
subject: MAGA is trying to gerrymander their way to victory
body:
Our democracy is under attack. We need to hold the line.
| | | | | [](https://hqigqzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0019LlYWusFJvAfd3JjoK3LrJW0l9r3by5K8A22N9yZ6VAq4iNCMia_xI3CVyrRVLe5L4u9ioeXjvnFBrSiQtX5vup7gBxNLJeqCuMLXe-5I-hYWB5jbs4Ckesshx74meJ3awQO0PoxAgbbxmKVL3dj0dDzV62qKg0pO1zELad7BpCMFnv89pk1JeRIjSrN2kbXeTSQWjrJdkr-mcKHDEqIcDOOiqJjmgedNI5QN5YnKD4=&c=4e5ndjYM6Y8qiA3a4T9HbeaHLmKW6I9Ap6BnlY8Pv7zHBJTB5ajAVg==&ch=dzrqZFLDJ7O_CxQ8Meu7ADBM3ikWHHKjJvJma6uGfazWymApjmlnSQ==)
---
| | | | Peter,
Politicians choosing their own voters, instead of voters choosing their politicians, is toxic to our democracy.
Texas Gov. Greg Abbott announced he will gerrymander Texas to stop Democrats from retaking control of the House this Midterm election. One party gerrymandering in the middle of an election cycle to redraw absurd districts that would deny the other party a majority in Congress is a vile act of cowardice and disdain for the American people.
MAGA has nothing but contempt towards the American people.
---
| | | | | [](https://hqigqzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0019LlYWusFJvAfd3JjoK3LrJW0l9r3by5K8A22N9yZ6VAq4iNCMia_xI3CVyrRVLe5L4u9ioeXjvnFBrSiQtX5vup7gBxNLJeqCuMLXe-5I-hYWB5jbs4Ckesshx74meJ3awQO0PoxAgbbxmKVL3dj0dDzV62qKg0pO1zELad7BpCMFnv89pk1JeRIjSrN2kbXeTSQWjrJdkr-mcKHDEqIcDOOiqJjmged4ydVZxKVr_0=&c=4e5ndjYM6Y8qiA3a4T9HbeaHLmKW6I9Ap6BnlY8Pv7zHBJTB5ajAVg==&ch=dzrqZFLDJ7O_CxQ8Meu7ADBM3ikWHHKjJvJma6uGfazWymApjmlnSQ==)
---
| | | | I'll get right to it: Texas' gerrymander upped the stakes for my race even higher.
We can't control what ultimately happens in Texas, a red state with a GOP majority in the legislature. But we can control what happens in critical races like mine. I am running in NH-01 is a swingy, purple district that votes for bold candidates who fight for solutions to real problems.
That is the fighting spirit I am bringing to this campaign--but if the MAGA GOP is going to pull out all the stops to defeat Democrats in states they control, we simply have to win here, and win big. The RNCC knows this - it's why they have already started attacking me.
The only path to victory in this must-win district is to put up a true fighter for the people.
I have served in the Army National Guard for years, standing up as a defense attorney for our troops and for our freedoms when they needed defending. And I will serve only the people in Congress, fighting for healthcare and for equal rights for every person in this country.
---
| | | | [But I need your help to do this. Can I count on your support to ensure that Democrats win one the most competitive districts in the country? This campaign is fueled by folks from across the country, and it will take a nationwide effort to win--a contribution of any amount right now makes a big impact:](https://hqigqzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0019LlYWusFJvAfd3JjoK3LrJW0l9r3by5K8A22N9yZ6VAq4iNCMia_xI3CVyrRVLe5AkIBdctfnqtVnfLQx_khibtjC6uMCGvLBSmZIuTmhGBy2AfR_9g8E7X_OcpzcLtMRbddWChscrRaut1w_4q3oseQNal4pZWT7oc2-R9_zRZD2NcD2-UFeDxKwy5OKtyV3VouizI4fO4zeXQWaBnd1_lVRq0hn2wa&c=4e5ndjYM6Y8qiA3a4T9HbeaHLmKW6I9Ap6BnlY8Pv7zHBJTB5ajAVg==&ch=dzrqZFLDJ7O_CxQ8Meu7ADBM3ikWHHKjJvJma6uGfazWymApjmlnSQ==)
---

That's the formula for online political fundraising. Campaigns send out lots of emails with little regard for how it damages our political system.

# [Panic! Everything's bad! Give me money!!](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=167817276&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY3ODE3Mjc2LCJpYXQiOjE3NTI2Njk5MzgsImV4cCI6MTc1NTI2MTkzOCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.9vSlkpCVMlL4Y5W6sRe7NsE107QT3Du80NHyQVwUGrg)
### That's the formula for online political fundraising. Campaigns send out lots of emails with little regard for how it damages our political system.
| | [Ben Samuels](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)
---
| Jul 16
---
| [](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=167817276&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY3ODE3Mjc2LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1MjY2OTkzOCwiZXhwIjoxNzU1MjYxOTM4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjQ0MDM5OCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.9TFwDMeUoOcWIAzrdIxhZ9c-7gaRt4Zog7fYPWITKVA&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=167817276&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY3ODE3Mjc2LCJpYXQiOjE3NTI2Njk5MzgsImV4cCI6MTc1NTI2MTkzOCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.9vSlkpCVMlL4Y5W6sRe7NsE107QT3Du80NHyQVwUGrg&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=167817276&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY3ODE3Mjc2LCJpYXQiOjE3NTI2Njk5MzgsImV4cCI6MTc1NTI2MTkzOCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.9vSlkpCVMlL4Y5W6sRe7NsE107QT3Du80NHyQVwUGrg)
---
| | [](https://substack..KhY7dghGZo6TcJlhqyY_oC6sG32Lu01Kor6aQbEwzgg?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/bensamuels/p/panic-give-me-money-fundraising-emails?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

If you've ever gotten a political fundraising email, you'll recognize that kind of subject line.
Having been a [candidate for Congress a few years ago](https://substack.com/redirect/e382bba6-7735-4d8d-98e7-7697bc79cfa6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), I got to see firsthand how text and email fundraising works. It's seedy, bad for our political system, and involves more lying and predatory behavior than most people realize.
At the bottom of this email, I've offered some thoughts on what you can do to protect yourselves and spare your inboxes.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTY3ODE3Mjc2LCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzUyNjY5OTM4LCJleHAiOjIwNjgyNDU5MzgsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.W8lzgX5xSIFieHr0xfhLLl3sbd2w90zsXx4IKK-0XVE?)
> _**Article summary:**_
> * _Neither political party has the authority to tell candidates who they can send emails to, or what those emails can say. That leads to more chaotic and extreme messaging._
> * _Your contact information is bought, sold, and traded between campaigns. In many cases, what campaigns send you is predatory and/or an outright lie._
> * _There are steps you can take to protect your personal information and weaken this fundraising ecosystem, which is damaging to political discourse in America._
>

### There's no such thing as "The Democrats"
A line I heard once that I really like: people conceive of political parties as marching bands, but they're really more like music festivals.
There's not a conductor telling everyone how to act, what to say, and what not to say. There's sort of an organizing committee via the DNC, but every candidate, advocacy group, and state party can communicate however they'd like. To complete the metaphor, everyone has their own stage.
Likewise, people think of "the Democrats" as a monolithic institution, with a master list of donors whose email addresses and phone numbers they give out. How it actually works: individual campaigns1 are free to message whoever they want, with any message they want, and with whatever frequency they want.
All of this sets up a [tragedy of the commons](https://substack.com/redirect/baa1d6fb-18c0-4c90-b9b4-5e2cd384c73d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Every campaign is acting rationally by trying to communicate with (and raise money from) people as much as they possibly can. **But politics suffers because people are spammed with frequent, low-quality, sensationalist, dishonest, and largely indistinguishable messages.**
The same thing is happening on the Republican side, even if the messaging itself is different. Campaigns in both parties are guilty here.
### How your information gets traded and sold
If you've ever wondered why it's so hard to get your name off of email lists after you've donated money or even if you've just signed up to volunteer, here's why.
For most campaigns, buried deep in their privacy agreement is the right to trade or sell your information however they want. They can sell their list of emails to generate more money for their campaign; they can buy them from other campaigns to try to raise money from new people.2
Here's a screenshot of an email I got from a campaign that was interested in selling me their email addresses. It's a pretty good reflection of how this stuff works.3
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/49edd164-781e-4dc3-af48-88a108bf00e7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This campaign was offering me hundreds of thousands of email addresses and phone numbers. That's a small fraction of the full political universe, where there's contact info for tens of millions of people.
This is how your info gets passed around, and why you end up receiving text messages like these, from candidates and campaigns you've never heard of.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/cebb74aa-f7bf-4779-9681-af6aa15ae3e5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
### Text/email fundraising sets up totally misaligned incentives
Said another way, no one here has any incentive to be a good steward of either people's contact information or of Democratic messaging generally.
1. **Campaigns** : Their goal is to raise as much money as possible, which means making their email lists as large as possible and hitting people up as frequently as possible. There's no reason to be worried about burnout.
2. **Email vendors** : Most campaigns pay an outside firm to draft and send emails on their behalf. (Rarely are they written in-house, which is why every email looks and sounds so freaking similar.) Their incentive is to justify their fees, which means that they're in the business of raising as much money as possible.
3. **ActBlue** : A lot of people think that ActBlue is part of the Democratic Party, but it's not. They have their own incentives as an independent organization. They make money by taking [3.95% of every transaction on their platform](https://substack.com/redirect/9d0a6b8e-663e-442e-9d7f-459abb503805?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg); for context, that is [meaningfully higher than standard credit card fees](https://substack.com/redirect/21f0b8c7-8d1c-4ef8-95b7-f22a958be9a4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).4

Notably: **email vendors and ActBlue have much stronger incentives to help campaigns raise money than to help campaigns win votes.** This is, for a host of reasons, bad.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=167817276&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY3ODE3Mjc2LCJpYXQiOjE3NTI2Njk5MzgsImV4cCI6MTc1NTI2MTkzOCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.9vSlkpCVMlL4Y5W6sRe7NsE107QT3Du80NHyQVwUGrg)
Why?
* Messaging that helps people raise money is distinct from messaging that actually helps people win swing voters and, therefore, win elections.
* It burns people out.
* It's borderline predatory.
* It ends up funneling money to candidates with absolutely no shot of winning and/or who don't need your money. Take the three text messages I shared above:
* [Annie Andrews](https://substack.com/redirect/7a6fa493-e5a8-4cc3-a784-4144d1652563?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is running in a [crowded Democratic primary](https://substack.com/redirect/0c8d0dc0-d8ec-40d3-b46a-0da594258ba4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for the U.S. Senate seat in South Carolina, where _any_ Democrat will very likely lose.
* [Maxwell Frost](https://substack.com/redirect/9750243a-5f54-4546-9299-5a060c03c37f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [Jasmine Crockett](https://substack.com/redirect/44407213-c837-42d3-8d11-96caacc3e9ad?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) represent heavily Democratic seats ([D+13](https://substack.com/redirect/8e5dcb2f-1cda-497c-9201-4ec22d49165f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [D+25](https://substack.com/redirect/a8530607-7365-4f57-a2b8-2ae645a4a454?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), respectively) and do not need your money.

### Messaging in text/email fundraising is all terrible
The [Archive of Political Emails](https://substack.com/redirect/de5aabe5-e11c-4bf2-9ac7-b56f0d47aec9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is a wonderful resource, because it tells you what people are receiving in their inboxes. A couple of recent examples, first from Democrats and liberal groups:
* "[a MAGA super PAC just launched a 7-figure smear campaign against Mikie](https://substack.com/redirect/368531a7-63aa-44fe-8d0d-a8101d1c9cec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)"
* "[Pam Bondi is at it again (loud opposition needed)](https://substack.com/redirect/f1b0e825-ae96-4aec-923f-6fcbc4ba6961?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)"
* "[If hell no was an option, that's how I would have voted.](https://substack.com/redirect/ac7b80fc-80a0-4759-9b54-b3791bde8163?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)"

And also from Republicans and conservative groups:
* "[Trump & DeSantis ERUPTED on the left's scheme to ROB YOU!](https://substack.com/redirect/fd6d2c22-6d8b-49d4-8262-abbd418201d0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)"
* "[BREAKING: James Comey PINNED with another crime?!](https://substack.com/redirect/16027288-0542-4b7f-a04b-168352666872?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)"
* From Matt Gaetz: "[EXONERATED.](https://substack.com/redirect/9a04c03e-e74c-4319-ae2a-f6e18feae923?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)"5

You know what undecided voters care about? It's not a MAGA super PAC and it's not James Comey.
**The[three things that swing voters cared about most](https://substack.com/redirect/7ac682db-2f6f-428a-8712-a6847cf53a07?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the 2024 presidential election: the economy, immigration, and social security/Medicare.**
These emails push extremism and actively make it harder to win more undecided voters. They advance a narrative that politics isn't about helping people, but that it's about tearing the other guy down. And reading these emails, maybe that narrative is right.
### A lot of email fundraising is just a grift
Here are a few examples of that:
1. Have you ever gotten a matching pledge from a campaign? ("If you give $1, a donor will match your donation!") Odds are [extremely high that's just a lie](https://substack.com/redirect/48367854-de5d-4ba4-b0d5-0fbb03bf76f8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
2. Fundraising tactics--from both political parties--have grown so deceptive that [it's literally bankrupting a lot of seniors](https://substack.com/redirect/10725b67-7b32-4dc6-b554-1c566b99b395?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). In the '23-'24 cycle, nearly 20,000 people gave more than 100 ActBlue donations, which doesn't look like responsible or rational behavior to me. One person in Washington state individually made more than 33,000 donations via ActBlue in that period.6
3. In 2022, Marcus Flowers ran for Congress against Marjorie Taylor Greene, [raised more than $16 million](https://substack.com/redirect/320be491-4ea6-4cec-b42f-396ca3fd2d28?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and [lost by more than 30%](https://substack.com/redirect/3d9e4732-942f-47fc-a023-18e2c4b08a69?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). How? His emails effectively tricked people into thinking that donating to him was a good use of their money. (It wasn't.)
4. The same thing happens on the Republican side; Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez's opponent in 2020 [raised nearly $10 million](https://substack.com/redirect/835e6051-61b6-46a9-afc5-08f932351322?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [lost the race by nearly 45%](https://substack.com/redirect/95c586e7-59a5-401d-a6b6-34c2b54111f2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

I wrote last time about [how hard it is to contextualize large numbers](https://substack.com/redirect/aaa8bad8-c24b-4158-80c0-5092e5418c9b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but: $10+ million is an extraordinarily large amount of money for an unwinnable U.S. House race. The [average ](https://substack.com/redirect/3188fd91-b8a1-4a76-85c4-d5ca5387f2c6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[winner](https://substack.com/redirect/3188fd91-b8a1-4a76-85c4-d5ca5387f2c6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ of a House race spent $2.79 million](https://substack.com/redirect/3188fd91-b8a1-4a76-85c4-d5ca5387f2c6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in 2022.7 Marcus Flowers raised and spent nearly 6× that.
Beyond any of that, though, are all of the outright lies. No, [JD Vance is not opening up a direct line of communication with you](https://substack.com/redirect/98753751-6ecd-4cb1-9a4c-877a2fc6fbfb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). A lot of emails are written to frighten people into action. For example:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6740569b-4819-4b25-9212-557660660b2d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The email on the left [won't get you a $5,000 refund check](https://substack.com/redirect/dce9596e-d7f2-4a02-bfe0-d5e8c702ef39?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It's a fundraising email from [Randy Fine](https://substack.com/redirect/eec0f163-c374-4681-ba6d-7627f93cd3dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a Congressman in Florida.
And as for the email on the right, no, [that's not how party memberships work](https://substack.com/redirect/135749c5-d5f6-4e2e-893f-965dc8f6567d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It's not even from the GOP--it's from [Nate Morris](https://substack.com/redirect/8239c7d0-ad80-4701-abba-784fb98d24ba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), who's running for the U.S. Senate in Kentucky.
Both Morris and Fine are trying to bamboozle people into giving them money.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=167817276&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY3ODE3Mjc2LCJpYXQiOjE3NTI2Njk5MzgsImV4cCI6MTc1NTI2MTkzOCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.9vSlkpCVMlL4Y5W6sRe7NsE107QT3Du80NHyQVwUGrg)
### What can you do about all of this?
Because I was a candidate in '22, I've been a beneficiary of this system; it's brought me a lot more exposure than I'd ever otherwise have.
But I look back with regret; I'm embarrassed by emails sent in my name. It was a lot of the same crap that I'm annoyed about every time it lands in my inbox.
It took leaving that world to understand how bad and embarrassing the whole setup is. In hindsight, I would've rather raised a little bit less money and held onto a little more dignity.
Here are my thoughts on what you can do about this:
1. **Be wary of any political email that you receive**. The Vice President of the United States isn't reaching out to you in a cold email or text. That matching pledge is probably made up. Approach emails with the same skepticism that you might any other cold call or cold email.
2. If you want to donate to candidates, if possible, **don 't give money via ActBlue**. [Checks are fading away](https://substack.com/redirect/2a0d8edb-3b65-43a6-871b-1fb89c16bd6d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but [lots of Americans still write checks periodically](https://substack.com/redirect/75d6d09a-aa73-4450-ade5-7e49a90a5bee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and that's how I do my political giving. If you give money by check, campaigns keep more of your donation,8 and they don't get your personal info.
3. **Think about where your money is best deployed**. Last year, I encouraged people to donate to [candidates and causes where support would go the furthest](https://substack.com/redirect/7f566a6c-2550-4f89-b6b1-f31de35fbfc2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).9 I'd love to see someone like Marjorie Taylor Greene lose, but she's in an extremely Republican seat; it's not going to happen. Money is better spent in more competitive races and districts.
4. If you must sign up for something online, **use fake email addresses and cell phone numbers**. You can get a fake email address on [sites like these](https://substack.com/redirect/b03eef06-4620-4f92-9426-412cec773e9b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and [Google Voice](https://substack.com/redirect/638d9837-1567-407e-bf01-dc68bd664db0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) works well for temporary phone numbers.

There's already too much mistrust in politics. Emails and texts from both political parties make it worse, and we should do what we can to knock down a system that drives extremism and misinformation.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTY3ODE3Mjc2LCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzUyNjY5OTM4LCJleHAiOjIwNjgyNDU5MzgsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.W8lzgX5xSIFieHr0xfhLLl3sbd2w90zsXx4IKK-0XVE?)
_**Feel free to share this post with someone who will find this interesting.** If you're reading this email because someone sent it to you, [please consider subscribing](https://substack.com/redirect/7b05ab7c-f6aa-4bb3-8923-e74e4183fe7e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)._
1
This is a pretty broad term that could include candidates running for office, ballot measures, advocacy groups building support, etc.
2
"Swaps," where a campaign trades emails with another campaign, are also quite common. (For example: a Candidate A in Virginia might give a Candidate B in Pennsylvania 1,000 email addresses for Pennsylvanians on their list, and in return, Candidate B would give 1,000 emails to Candidate A for people in Virginia. All of that would be totally cost neutral.)
3
To be really precise here: this is a screenshot of two separate emails that I received. I have combined them into one email for three different reasons:
* To anonymize which campaign this came from.
* To share more pertinent information.
* To format it in a way that it works better with Substack's interface.

Nothing about the text has been edited, nor does it change any of the substance. But I want to be clear that it's not a _verbatim_ email.
For context on what everything in that screenshot means:
* Donors vs. non-donors/prospects: People who have donated money to this campaign vs. people who haven't.
* Active vs. inactive: Whether you've recently opened emails, clicked on links, etc.

4
It's even higher than [Square](https://substack.com/redirect/23ee005a-55c3-4f77-8812-e2b1323006cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which charges [2.9% per transaction](https://substack.com/redirect/23ee005a-55c3-4f77-8812-e2b1323006cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (plus a fixed 30¢ per-transaction fee). I don't know the exact structure of WinRed, the Republican equivalent of ActBlue, but I think it's substantially the same.
5
Maybe this one is just wishful thinking on his part.
6
I'm not linking to sources here, because it includes people's names and home addresses. Bulk data is available [here](https://substack.com/redirect/30c082c0-2050-482d-a0ff-722d02e89b12?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from the Federal Elections Commission, which makes all of this sort of information public. I dusted off my long-dormant SQL knowledge to analyze 100+ million rows of data, which is my source for the stats above.
7
Right here in Missouri, Jess Piper [raised nearly $300,000](https://substack.com/redirect/f5faf524-6b10-4a97-b1f2-e9550e7e301e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for [a State House race she lost by more than 50%](https://substack.com/redirect/d4584e55-d4a9-4ad7-8a3f-ce9ad0e52ebb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Like Marcus Flowers' race, that's an extraordinarily large amount of money for an unwinnable Missouri State House race.
8
Because ActBlue doesn't take that 3.95% cut, which adds up over the course of a campaign that raises millions of dollars.
9
Relevant to all of this: I set up the ActBlue link in a way that campaigns didn't get your email or cell information.

Campaigns send out lots of emails with little regard for how it damages our political system. | | | Jul 16 | | | | | | | | | | | | If you've ever gotten a political fundraising email, you'll recognize that kind of subject line. Having been a , I got to see firsthand how text and email fundraising works. It's seedy, bad for our political system, and involves more lying and predatory behavior than most people realize. At the bottom of this email, I've offered some thoughts on what you can do to protect yourselves and spare your inboxes. ? Article summary: Neither political party has the authority to tell candidates who they can send emails to, or what those emails can say. That leads to more chaotic and extreme messaging. Your contact information is bought, sold, and traded between campaigns. In many cases, what campaigns send you is predatory and/or an outright lie. There are steps you can take to protect your personal information and weaken this fundraising ecosystem, which is damaging to political discourse in America. There's no such thing as "The Democrats" A line I heard once that I really like: people conceive of political parties as marching bands, but they're really more like music festivals. There's not a conductor telling everyone how to act, what to say, and what not to say. There's sort of an organizing committee via the DNC, but every candidate, advocacy group, and state party can communicate however they'd like. To complete the metaphor, everyone has their own stage. Likewise, people think of "the Democrats" as a monolithic institution, with a master list of donors whose email addresses and phone numbers they give out. How it actually works: individual campaigns1 are free to message whoever they want, with any message they want, and with whatever frequency they want. All of this sets up a . Every campaign is acting rationally by trying to communicate with (and raise money from) people as much as they possibly can. But politics suffers because people are spammed with frequent, low-quality, sensationalist, dishonest, and largely indistinguishable messages. The same thing is happening on the Republican side, even if the messaging itself is different. Campaigns in both parties are guilty here. How your information gets traded and sold If you've ever wondered why it's so hard to get your name off of email lists after you've donated money or even if you've just signed up to volunteer, here's why. For most campaigns, buried deep in their privacy agreement is the right to trade or sell your information however they want. They can sell their list of emails to generate more money for their campaign; they can buy them from other campaigns to try to raise money from new people.2 Here's a screenshot of an email I got from a campaign that was interested in selling me their email addresses. It's a pretty good reflection of how this stuff works.3 | | ---|---|--- This campaign was offering me hundreds of thousands of email addresses and phone numbers. That's a small fraction of the full political universe, where there's contact info for tens of millions of people. This is how your info gets passed around, and why you end up receiving text messages like these, from candidates and campaigns you've never heard of. | | ---|---|--- Text/email fundraising sets up totally misaligned incentives Said another way, no one here has any incentive to be a good steward of either people's contact information or of Democratic messaging generally. 1. Campaigns : Their goal is to raise as much money as possible, which means making their email lists as large as possible and hitting people up as frequently as possible. There's no reason to be worried about burnout. 2. Email vendors : Most campaigns pay an outside firm to draft and send emails on their behalf. (Rarely are they written in-house, which is why every email looks and sounds so freaking similar.) Their incentive is to justify their fees, which means that they're in the business of raising as much money as possible. 3. ActBlue : A lot of people think that ActBlue is part of the Democratic Party, but it's not. They have their own incentives as an independent organization. They make money by taking ; for context, that is Notably: email vendors and ActBlue have much stronger incentives to help campaigns raise money than to help campaigns win votes. This is, for a host of reasons, bad. Why? Messaging that helps people raise money is distinct from messaging that actually helps people win swing voters and, therefore, win elections. It burns people out. It's borderline predatory. It ends up funneling money to candidates with absolutely no shot of winning and/or who don't need your money. Take the three text messages I shared above: is running in a for the U.S. Senate seat in South Carolina, where any Democrat will very likely lose. and represent heavily Democratic seats ( and , respectively) and do not need your money. Messaging in text/email fundraising is all terrible The is a wonderful resource, because it tells you what people are receiving in their inboxes. A couple of recent examples, first from Democrats and liberal groups: "" "" "" And also from Republicans and conservative groups: "" "" From Matt Gaetz: ""5 You know what undecided voters care about? It's not a MAGA super PAC and it's not James Comey. The in the 2024 presidential election: the economy, immigration, and social security/Medicare. These emails push extremism and actively make it harder to win more undecided voters. They advance a narrative that politics isn't about helping people, but that it's about tearing the other guy down. And reading these emails, maybe that narrative is right. A lot of email fundraising is just a grift Here are a few examples of that: 1. Have you ever gotten a matching pledge from a campaign? ("If you give $1, a donor will match your donation!") Odds are . 2. Fundraising tactics--from both political parties--have grown so deceptive that . In the '23-'24 cycle, nearly 20,000 people gave more than 100 ActBlue donations, which doesn't look like responsible or rational behavior to me. One person in Washington state individually made more than 33,000 donations via ActBlue in that period.6 3. In 2022, Marcus Flowers ran for Congress against Marjorie Taylor Greene, , and . How? His emails effectively tricked people into thinking that donating to him was a good use of their money. (It wasn't.) 4. The same thing happens on the Republican side; Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez's opponent in 2020 and . I wrote last time about , but: $10+ million is an extraordinarily large amount of money for an unwinnable U.S. House race. The in 2022.7 Marcus Flowers raised and spent nearly 6× that. Beyond any of that, though, are all of the outright lies. No, . A lot of emails are written to frighten people into action. For example: | | ---|---|--- The email on the left . It's a fundraising email from , a Congressman in Florida. And as for the email on the right, no, . It's not even from the GOP--it's from , who's running for the U.S. Senate in Kentucky. Both Morris and Fine are trying to bamboozle people into giving them money. What can you do about all of this? Because I was a candidate in '22, I've been a beneficiary of this system; it's brought me a lot more exposure than I'd ever otherwise have. But I look back with regret; I'm embarrassed by emails sent in my name. It was a lot of the same crap that I'm annoyed about every time it lands in my inbox. It took leaving that world to understand how bad and embarrassing the whole setup is. In hindsight, I would've rather raised a little bit less money and held onto a little more dignity. Here are my thoughts on what you can do about this: 1. Be wary of any political email that you receive. The Vice President of the United States isn't reaching out to you in a cold email or text. That matching pledge is probably made up. Approach emails with the same skepticism that you might any other cold call or cold email. 2. If you want to donate to candidates, if possible, don 't give money via ActBlue. , but , and that's how I do my political giving. If you give money by check, campaigns keep more of your donation,8 and they don't get your personal info. 3. Think about where your money is best deployed. Last year, I encouraged people to donate to I'd love to see someone like Marjorie Taylor Greene lose, but she's in an extremely Republican seat; it's not going to happen. Money is better spent in more competitive races and districts. 4. If you must sign up for something online, use fake email addresses and cell phone numbers. You can get a fake email address on , and works well for temporary phone numbers. There's already too much mistrust in politics. Emails and texts from both political parties make it worse, and we should do what we can to knock down a system that drives extremism and misinformation. ? name: Senator Rand Paul
subject: FW: End of the UN Arms Treaty in sight
body:
[unsubscribe](https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xul=1nmc5a54x67zfmb8icvorwpih3oizkk7a3pg0qu08t7r18g2dcc823wnprd4r79e9gu077z5u0zhx7slxkmfzkhgosjd75nnray2rf8ewpst7xbdtbbo&__stmp=szht14&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh)
---
Please read the message below from our sponsor, the National Association for Gun Rights.

Steve Thompson, Editor

* * *

|
---
| | | | | | |
---
|

Dear Fellow Patriot,

The United Nations’ “Small Arms Treaty” has been the Gun Control Lobby’s crown jewel since Barack Obama sent it to the U.S. Senate and insisted we ratify it a decade ago. Year after year, we refused.

_But each and every day, the UN is getting closer to implementing the terms of this dangerous Treaty’s provisions here in the United States even without ratification_.

**And it’s all being done in secret by the UN’s allies in the Washington, D.C. “Swamp.”**

The good news is, with the big changes in Washington due to the 2024 election, you and I have a chance to stop the Gun Control Lobby cold.

But it’s an opportunity that won’t last forever.

That’s why – as Dudley Brown, President of the National Association for Gun Rights, explains in the message below – **[it’s absolutely critical you and I act now](https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=yf6azf6ckk8hdcwulv2vc9xklh69dhvgch1qt26ptsbrrrwynj6bfez42u6zfh4drv99ih4uzcisbt8q40bdc4wenw8xprkq97e0qbikx2z15gynceba8wd8t00xrsmckhib6nboe5fpc25p76mzuvyy0n8fpkeoxtdfbrcw43t044znw413huy2x7rpkxk&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=szht14&__onlt=h)**!

**Sincerely,

The Hon. Rand Paul
United States Senator (R-KY)**

* * *

Dear Fellow Patriot,

**There is a sword hanging over the head of every American gun owner**.

I’m referring to the UN “Small Arms Treaty” -- the first stage of the UN’s global gun control agenda.

_I’ve stared these global gun banners in the face and told them what they could do with their precious “Small Arms Treaty,” though I knew the UN would NOT like me crashing their “Gun Ban Club” in Geneva_.

**_I told UN bureaucrats you and I will NEVER surrender to their anti-gun schemes!_**

When I told these anti-gun international bureaucrats they were walking in the footsteps of every 20th century murderous dictator who disarmed their citizens to shuffle them off to death camps, I guess my words hit a little “close to home.”

All they could do in response was squirm in their seats and laugh nervously...

But while you or I or even President Trump can tell these gun grabbers what we think of their treaty, only the U.S. Senate can remove the treaty document from the Senate agenda.

**[That’s why I’m counting on you to sign your “END THE UN GUN BAN” petition to your U.S. Senators IMMEDIATELY](https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=yf6azf6ckk8hdcwulv2vc9xklh69dhvgch1qt26ptsbrrrwynj6bfez42u6zfh4drv99ih4uzcisbt8q40bdc4wenw8xprkq97e0qbikx2z15gynceba8wd8t00xrsmckhib6nboe5fpc25p76mzuvyy0n8fpkeoxtdfbrcw43t044znw413huy2x7rpkxk&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=szht14&__onlt=h)**.

As you’ll see, this petition insists your Senators act to oppose and reject the UN “Small Arms Treaty” and send it back to the President where it can be formally and finally trashed.

But until they do, the UN “Small Arms Treaty” will remain pending in the Senate, and ratification by a future Senate is all that’s needed for this gun grab to become reality.

_And that’s REGARDLESS of who occupies the White House or their position on the Treaty_!

With this Treaty, the UN’s goal is to DESTROY the legal buying, owning, manufacturing, and selling of pistols, rifles, and shotguns by FORCING: **> > _ALL countries involved in the export of firearms -- including the United States -- to establish a “National Control System_.”** This is nothing more than GUN REGISTRATION, plain and simple -- the first step toward outright GUN CONFISCATION; **> > _ALL firearms manufacturers who seek to export firearms to ask for permission and report on how they believe the guns they sell will be used_.** The UN ultimately wants final say on whether or not any firearms transfer should be allowed. Period. After all, they are fixated on keeping guns out of the hands of the “wrong” people; and **> > _ALL Treaty countries to allow firearms manufacturers to be sued for crimes committed with their products_.** Imagine how outrageous it would be if Ford was sued because an F-150 owner ran a red light and caused a crash! That’s what gun grabbers want to do with firearms because they know this could END gun manufacturing. This is exactly what Mexico is attempting to do in their lawsuit against Smith & Wesson.
**_The UN’s ultimate plan for all civilian-owned guns:_Incinerate them_._**

It’s hard to see how the Second Amendment could survive in the United States were the one-world socialists at the UN to get their way.

Of course, that’s their goal.

_To them, your Second Amendment freedoms are a threat to their grand “utopian” designs_.

Meanwhile, enemies like Red China are so eager to see every American disarmed, they’re pouring money into the UN “Small Arms Treaty” hand-over-fist.

So, it’s no wonder why they’re hell-bent on forcing the U.S. to submit to this globalist gun control scam -- and why we must defeat it!

**[I’m counting on you to help beat the UN by signing your “END THE UN GUN BAN” petition to urge your U.S. Senators to end all support for the UN Gun Ban and send it back to the White House right away](https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=yf6azf6ckk8hdcwulv2vc9xklh69dhvgch1qt26ptsbrrrwynj6bfez42u6zfh4drv99ih4uzcisbt8q40bdc4wenw8xprkq97e0qbikx2z15gynceba8wd8t00xrsmckhib6nboe5fpc25p76mzuvyy0n8fpkeoxtdfbrcw43t044znw413huy2x7rpkxk&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=szht14&__onlt=h)**.

If we don’t dispose of the treaty quickly, the gun grabbers will leap to ratify the Treaty in the U.S. Senate the moment they have the votes, no matter who the President is or what their stance is on the treaty.

_That’s why we have to mount a massive campaign to send this Treaty back to the President NOW so he can kill the Treaty as promised, before it’s too late_.

The great news is, just doing so puts you and me in a win/win situation.

Either we get the 60 votes we need to finally KILL this Treaty...

... Or anti-gun U.S. Senators will be on record in support of arguably the **WORST anti-gun initiative** to come before either chamber of Congress in decades.

Believe me.

_U.S. Senators who vote to allow international bureaucrats to trample on our gun rights will pay the price at the ballot box_.

Just like they did in 2014 after their all-out push for gun control in the wake of the Newtown tragedy, and in 2016 after Hillary Clinton’s full embrace of every anti-gun policy imaginable, and in **2024 when four anti-gun democrats went down in flames, and four more barely hung on by the skin of their teeth**.

_But if we don’t fight now, it could be the gun grabbers who get the last laugh -- even if it takes them years to regain control of the U.S. Senate_!

After all, gun grabbers have been battling for this scheme since President Bill Clinton’s administration.

_They’re going to do whatever it takes to make their global gun control crown jewel a reality_.

They’re clearly in this for the long haul.

Are you?

**[Click here to sign your “END THE UN GUN BAN” Petition and insist the U.S. Senate vote to return the treaty to President Trump so he can send it to the trash where it belongs](https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=yf6azf6ckk8hdcwulv2vc9xklh69dhvgch1qt26ptsbrrrwynj6bfez42u6zfh4drv99ih4uzcisbt8q40bdc4wenw8xprkq97e0qbikx2z15gynceba8wd8t00xrsmckhib6nboe5fpc25p76mzuvyy0n8fpkeoxtdfbrcw43t044znw413huy2x7rpkxk&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=szht14&__onlt=h)**.

Sadly, the UN “Small Arms Treaty” is now only the beginning of the problem.

_Under Joe Biden’s direction, one-world bureaucrats are even now pushing key parts of these gun controls deep into every part of government_.

On top of the Biden ATF’s anti-gun executive actions, a headline from The Hill showed how **“Democrats’ latest backdoor plan to limit gun ownership”** involves new State Department “sanctions” designed to drive the price of ammo up by BANNING imports.

_That’s the same State Department which NAGR caught red-handed restarting the funnel of your tax dollars into funding the UN “Small Arms Treaty_.”

Worse, since day one of the COVID-19 outbreak, our court system has said yes to almost any politician cooking up any unconstitutional idea so long as it’s under the guise of “public health.”

None of us want any more of that.

Still, now the Center for Disease Control (CDC) is pushing a new Euro-centric gun control framework that says _“Using a public health approach is essential to addressing firearm violence and keeping people safe and healthy.”_

_That’s why insisting that your U.S. Senators vote to DISPOSE of the UN’s “Small Arms Treaty” is not enough_.

We must insist that the U.S. Senate help President Trump root out anti-gun policies pushed by Joe Biden’s government and ensure that NOT ONE TAXPAYER CENT is spent on implementing any part of this Treaty or what it stands for.

But I’m counting on your help to send that message -- before it’s all swept under the rug by the bureaucrats running for cover.

**[First, please sign your “END THE UN GUN BAN” Petition to both of your U.S. Senators at once](https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=yf6azf6ckk8hdcwulv2vc9xklh69dhvgch1qt26ptsbrrrwynj6bfez42u6zfh4drv99ih4uzcisbt8q40bdc4wenw8xprkq97e0qbikx2z15gynceba8wd8t00xrsmckhib6nboe5fpc25p76mzuvyy0n8fpkeoxtdfbrcw43t044znw413huy2x7rpkxk&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=szht14&__onlt=h)**.

Once you have signed your petition, I am counting on your support to help the National Association for Gun Rights: 1) _Contact and mobilize_ up to 10 million pro-Second Amendment Americans using mail, email and phone banks to turn up maximum pressure on the U.S. Senate; 2) _Launch a full-scale online effort_ , including internet ads, op-eds, text messages, and video updates, working highly-trafficked blogs and using social media to generate even more heat to ensure the so-called “Small Arms Treaty” can be returned to the White House where President Trump can finish it off; and 3) _Craft hard-hitting radio_ , newspaper and TV ads, which we’ll be ready to unleash at a moment’s notice if we can raise the resources to fund them. Of course, a program of this scale won’t be cheap.

But as I explained, doing nothing would only play right into the gun grabbers’ hands.

**[So along with your signed petition, could you please consider a generous contribution of $100 TODAY](https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=37t93ovur1rhsg69r4pmzpc5tr2wtlpt9qtyguh9ddzfew3jfeda6pl7x13caufiabpvx4odu6z7oskdcg2d7cfl98pef7ehri95rqge5x6vda4xir060w7m7hsu2h43s6jldolrr4djaaiclc847owoe4ojny8foduprknp85nyjrjapeevbin424419n&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=szht14&__onlt=h)**?

I know that’s a lot to ask for, believe me.

But the fight to reclaim our stolen freedoms is here, Fellow Patriot. It’s on. There’s no time to wait.

So please act -- and agree to give generously.

**[But if $100 is just too much -- even given the urgency of the situation -- please agree to $30, or at least $15 TODAY](https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=37t93ovur1rhsg69r4pmzpc5tr2wtlpt9qtyguh9ddzfew3jfeda6pl7x13caufiabpvx4odu6z7oskdcg2d7cfl98pef7ehri95rqge5x6vda4xir060w7m7hsu2h43s6jldolrr4djaaiclc847owoe4ojny8foduprknp85nyjrjapeevbin424419n&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=szht14&__onlt=h)**.

_If you and I don’t fight back, they’ll be back again to keep stealing our freedoms one bite at a time_.

Whatever you can do, please act IMMEDIATELY. There’s no time to waste.

**For Freedom,

Dudley Brown
President
National Association for Gun Rights **

P.S. As long as the UN “Small Arms Treaty” is pending in the Senate, ratification is all that’s needed for this gun grab to become reality. And that’s REGARDLESS of who occupies the White House or their position on the Treaty!

This petition insists your Senators vote to reject the UN “Small Arms Treaty” and send it back to the President where he can kill it.

**[So will you please sign your “END THE UN GUN BAN” Petition IMMEDIATELY and agree to your most generous contribution of $100, $30 or at least $15 TODAY](https://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=yf6azf6ckk8hdcwulv2vc9xklh69dhvgch1qt26ptsbrrrwynj6bfez42u6zfh4drv99ih4uzcisbt8q40bdc4wenw8xprkq97e0qbikx2z15gynceba8wd8t00xrsmckhib6nboe5fpc25p76mzuvyy0n8fpkeoxtdfbrcw43t044znw413huy2x7rpkxk&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=szht14&__onlt=h)**?

# Opinion:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f1bda08c-dfd5-400b-b5b8-01f6e5129cc4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**By Daniel Turner**
I apologize. I am slow to finish this series... but it is not from a lack of interest. Besides a full-time job, I have a full-time farm, and summer is the busiest time.
So let me continue with the last few big ideas. You can find the first six **[here](https://substack.com/redirect/a9035a06-66af-4bd8-8434-293a9afe5778?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**. The next four are ideas which will bring structural change to the nation forever.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..NyChY2HD-n17V5jhjs21AKFQ1cpwBB-3VZsjfd0SG8I?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=168465666)
## Seven: The Quarterly Income Tax Payment
Convenience has done a great disservice to the nation of taxpayers. We have become accustomed to our payroll automatically deducting from our paychecks the government's cut, and we've become accustomed to this absurd, abusive, amoral amount disappearing into the "pay your fair share" black hole.
If we really want fiscal responsibility... if we really want government accountability and to end the waste... then we need a structural change to our taxation, and I propose a very simple solution: quarterly tax payments.
Let's use an example. An employee in Virginia who makes $75,000 a year **[would owe the federal government](https://substack.com/redirect/3badf526-dc6b-4de1-b0f7-6ce66423d24c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** roughly $18,500 in taxes. If he's paid twice a month, then every paycheck sees an automatic $770 deduction.
I propose no deduction. Full paycheck with the full amount BUT at the end of the quarter, said employee would be obligated to send in a check for the same amount. In this case, every quarter, our employee must write the IRS a check for $4,620.
Nothing- absolutely nothing- will make someone a fiscal hawk faster than having to write so large a check so frequently. That check-writing employee would scrutinize every Congressional appropriation and every foreign aid package. News stories **[like](https://substack.com/redirect/aaa3cc88-0a8c-42e2-bfa3-d61cb1b4c2d8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** "the Federal Reserve's HQ renovation is running $700 million over budget" hit a lot harder when you are writing a $4,620 check every three months. Your Congressman's bill to perpetually fund the war in {insert country here} is harder embrace at a Town Hall, a Town Hall, which, you are now way more likely to attend because that Congressman expects you to write another $4,620 check in three more months.
I'm not even proposing lowering taxes or raising them, but such a structural change will bring a level of citizen involvement Congress does not want. Sure, the current deduction model is convenient... but convenient for whom?
**A message from our sponsor (piece continues below)**

**Together with Natural Health Response**
# [RFK Jr Issues 2025 Warning [Seniors Most At Risk?]](https://substack.com/redirect/26f7d4c3-c51c-41e3-bae5-b1613a040aee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/26f7d4c3-c51c-41e3-bae5-b1613a040aee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Dear Reader,
For years liberals have called RFK Jr a _**QUACK**_ ... an _**ALARMIST**_ ... and a _**CONSPIRACY THEORIST**_.
But now it appears _**HE WAS RIGHT**_ about a COVID lab leak...
He was _**RIGHT**_ about microplastics...
And he was _**RIGHT**_ about FDA corruption.
Now, he's issued **[a terrifying new warning](https://substack.com/redirect/26f7d4c3-c51c-41e3-bae5-b1613a040aee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** that Americans need to hear in 2025.
Millions are in the crosshairs...
And if you were born before Jan 1st, 1965, **[THIS](https://substack.com/redirect/26f7d4c3-c51c-41e3-bae5-b1613a040aee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** affects you most of all.
Get the full facts **[here](https://substack.com/redirect/26f7d4c3-c51c-41e3-bae5-b1613a040aee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**.
P.S. Liberals will call **[THIS](https://substack.com/redirect/26f7d4c3-c51c-41e3-bae5-b1613a040aee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** crazy too, until they see **[the data](https://substack.com/redirect/26f7d4c3-c51c-41e3-bae5-b1613a040aee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**. Learn why RFK Jr is warning Americans about this emerging health crisis before it's too late. Watch **[this video](https://substack.com/redirect/26f7d4c3-c51c-41e3-bae5-b1613a040aee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** now.
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/26f7d4c3-c51c-41e3-bae5-b1613a040aee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
**(Piece continues)**
## Eight: No More Government Cheese
Once the citizen realizes he is the government's ATM, he will call for an end of government philanthropy understanding that there is no such thing as government philanthropy, but only confiscation of one's property redistributed as government deems fit.
"This is a program which provides shoes for the homeless". No. "Under this program pregnant migrants can receive...". No. "This program offers jobs training for...". No.
Even intentionality will not save these programs. Sorry, Kant.
America is a kind and generous nation, but right now, as our $37 trillion debt can attest, we have far too many people who live off the government. These programs exist forever, costing more, fixing nothing, but creating government dependency for generations.
A woman was finally cut off from food stamps **[after multiple TikTok](https://substack.com/redirect/4d469f40-d68e-4dcb-9601-b9a413a6639b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** (TikTok is poison, FYI) videos bragging about her $3000 shopping bills at your expense. "Food is way expensive. Who wants to keep spending money on food? It's too much," she said.
That woman has been led to believe she has a right to your taxes. And it is time we corrected that hierarchy forever.
"What about the children?" No. "What about her health and wellbeing?" No. The answer is, it must be, it has to be: "No".
Private philanthropy can cover needs. If that is not enough, people will do what you and I do which is find a way to make money and contribute to society.
"I thought you were a Christian! I thought you were pro-life!" Don't let them use your values against you. Don't let them think you are obligated to contribute more because they refuse to take less. No. The answer is, it must be, no. As heartless as it sounds, and at the risk of being accused of being the most awful man in Congress, every one of the government cheese programs must be closed.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..NyChY2HD-n17V5jhjs21AKFQ1cpwBB-3VZsjfd0SG8I?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=168465666)
## Nine: The Senior Citizen Capital Gains Tax Exemption
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f1bda08c-dfd5-400b-b5b8-01f6e5129cc4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The One Big Beautiful Bill (now law) has a change in the tax code exempting social security from taxation, correcting one of the few mistakes of the Reagan Presidency. Until 1984, social security was tax free, but the Greenspan Commission worried the program was financially insecure, and taxation was easier than reform. And yes, Social Security must be reformed.
But one step at time: No capital gains taxes after your 70th birthday.
Seniors are living longer than ever and that means they will need money longer. The most gracious thing we can do for our seniors is allow them to keep all their wealth.
Seniors who bought stock in the 80's get to keep 100% of their profits. Seniors who bought their home during the Vietnam War for $34,000 and it's now worth $850,000, should be able to sell and downsize, keeping every penny of the capital gains.
That is more money for assisted living or for healthcare, home aids, bathroom remodels to add grab bars and high toilets, stair lifts... let seniors keep their money for a dignified end of life, and most importantly, so they don't need the government's support.
Now, once this is implemented with seniors... we will be able to implement it universally. Because the true capital gains tax rate should, of course, be 0%.
The value of every good which appreciates should remain with the owner of that good. The government has no right to confiscate a percentage. It is wrong. It is deeply immoral. But I also know we're not going to get this to pass for everyone... so let's start with the seniors who do need it more urgently, and once enough people see Grandma sell her home with zero capital gains tax, they will want capital gains tax eliminated for themselves, too. That will be a great day for freedom and property.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..NyChY2HD-n17V5jhjs21AKFQ1cpwBB-3VZsjfd0SG8I?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=168465666)
## Ten: The Itemized Tax Returns
"I need an itemized receipt" is in my lexicon. Probably yours, too. Accountants and CFOs and everyone in between who looks at your books wants to know what was actually purchased.
If your contractor handed you a $100,000 bill, you'd ask for an itemized receipt. "Wait, this line here, what is a tile storage fee, and why is it $1200?"
If we are going to clean up the government's books, make our citizens fiscally savvy and responsible, the government owes us an itemized receipt. After all, the IRS asks one of us, does it not?
What percentage of your taxes goes to defense? What percentage goes to Medicaid? Ukraine? Debt servicing? Government salaries?
This is not even hard to do. Our previous example of the Virginia resident who earns $75,000 a year and pays $18,500 in taxes can easily get an itemized receipt. How shocked will he be when he sees that he is still in the red $40,000 because government overspent yet again.
Department of Commerce: 1.5%. "What is the Department of Commerce?" our friend may ask his Senator at a Town Hall.
"Senator, based on my itemized tax receipt, 2.75% of my tax liability went towards 'miscellaneous'. Can you explain that to me?"
"Senator, 25% of my tax liability went towards Medicaid. You voted to allow illegal aliens to receive Medicaid..." imagine the rest of that conversation. Woof... good luck, Senator. I'll tell you this, my yahoo Senators from Virginia would not be able to answer these questions if they ever held a Town Hall.
And what we are doing with these proposal, these last four which are more economic, the previous ones which are cultural, is we are creating an educated, involved electorate. We are creating a nation of responsible and active citizens, not passive victims observing the government and the culture from their sofa, developing an unhealthy detachment from both.
I am not an anarchist. Government does have a role, and we can shape it so the role it plays makes us a smarter, savvier, nation. Decades of deliberate reshaping of government have created distance. These proposals throw Americans in the mix of these battles for their dollars and their souls, their liberty, property, and prosperity.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..NyChY2HD-n17V5jhjs21AKFQ1cpwBB-3VZsjfd0SG8I?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=168465666)
* * *
**Ten ideas.** If I go to Congress someday... I will propose them all. Look at that, a campaign platform and I'm not even a candidate. And so much better a campaign platform than the hollow rhetoric "put hope over fear, build bridges not walls". Our current political candidates sound like bad poets more than serious statesmen.
And I'll keep talking about other issues: taxes, religion, culture, race, history... I'll bring up the taboo and the controversial. Heck, next time I testify before Congress I want some lame Congressman to hold up one of my articles on a posterboard and excoriate me for having the guts to address the things he's too cowardly to fix. (That's a nod to **[Rep. Garcia](https://substack.com/redirect/c25db76a-bc56-469e-ad0f-f8017a70e5a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** who did just that to me a few weeks ago...)
We can fix everything. But it has to start with an idea, and sometimes a brawl. Until then...
We keep fighting. Honor and courage.

That will be a great day for freedom and property. Ten: The Itemized Tax Returns "I need an itemized receipt" is in my lexicon. Probably yours, too. Accountants and CFOs and everyone in between who looks at your books wants to know what was actually purchased. If your contractor handed you a $100,000 bill, you'd ask for an itemized receipt. "Wait, this line here, what is a tile storage fee, and why is it $1200?" If we are going to clean up the government's books, make our citizens fiscally savvy and responsible, the government owes us an itemized receipt. After all, the IRS asks one of us, does it not? What percentage of your taxes goes to defense? What percentage goes to Medicaid? Ukraine? Debt servicing? Government salaries? This is not even hard to do. Our previous example of the Virginia resident who earns $75,000 a year and pays $18,500 in taxes can easily get an itemized receipt. How shocked will he be when he sees that he is still in the red $40,000 because government overspent yet again. Department of Commerce: 1.5%. "What is the Department of Commerce?" our friend may ask his Senator at a Town Hall. "Senator, based on my itemized tax receipt, 2.75% of my tax liability went towards 'miscellaneous'. Can you explain that to me?" "Senator, 25% of my tax liability went towards Medicaid. You voted to allow illegal aliens to receive Medicaid..." imagine the rest of that conversation. Woof... good luck, Senator. I'll tell you this, my yahoo Senators from Virginia would not be able to answer these questions if they ever held a Town Hall. And what we are doing with these proposal, these last four which are more economic, the previous ones which are cultural, is we are creating an educated, involved electorate. We are creating a nation of responsible and active citizens, not passive victims observing the government and the culture from their sofa, developing an unhealthy detachment from both. I am not an anarchist. Government does have a role, and we can shape it so the role it plays makes us a smarter, savvier, nation. Decades of deliberate reshaping of government have created distance. These proposals throw Americans in the mix of these battles for their dollars and their souls, their liberty, property, and prosperity. Ten ideas. If I go to Congress someday... I will propose them all. Look at that, a campaign platform and I'm not even a candidate. And so much better a campaign platform than the hollow rhetoric "put hope over fear, build bridges not walls". Our current political candidates sound like bad poets more than serious statesmen. And I'll keep talking about other issues: taxes, religion, culture, race, history... I'll bring up the taboo and the controversial. Heck, next time I testify before Congress I want some lame Congressman to hold up one of my articles on a posterboard and excoriate me for having the guts to address the things he's too cowardly to fix. (That's a nod to who did just that to me a few weeks ago...) We can fix everything. But it has to start with an idea, and sometimes a brawl. Until then... We keep fighting. Honor and courage. Daniel Turner is the founder and executive director of Power The Future, a national nonprofit organization that advocates for American energy jobs. Contact him at daniel@powerthefuture.com and follow him on Twitter @DanielTurnerPTF * PolitiBrawl is a reader-supported publication. body:
My fate is in your hands...

| [](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zm91cv4bl4ag84grrp0mvvlr7acpgs1o5kjqocgo_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cdgeaoj6cph2bs)
---
Dear Friend,
---
The Radical Democrats think they’ve finally got me.
---
**You see, a Democrat Attorney General is making me stand in a rigged trial for bogus state-level crimes, because she knows that not even the most powerful man on the face of the planet – President Trump – can save me with a _federal_ pardon in _state_ court.**
---
_I’m left with no escape and no lifeline, especially after they’ve already STRIPPED me of my law licenses!_
---
But even though the most powerful man in the country legally cannot step in to save me from being **JAILED** for the rest of my life, the most powerful force absolutely can: _THE AMERICAN PEOPLE_.
---
With my back against the wall, I’m turning to the _ONE_ _THING_ that can save me: _**the collective voices of MILLIONS of patriots like YOU.**_
---
[**Please help me reach my life-saving goal (_truly!_) today by immediately adding your name to “THE PEOPLE’S PARDON OF RUDY GIULIANI” as citizens unite to call for my freedom and to DROP ALL CHARGES against me.**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z5essp82uirk7n7el4k8m00ugads4j9j4fj46300_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cdgeaoj6cph2bs)

[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z56sv19k3ljad2rp921bna5v2voqduocp1uq0e7o_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cdgeaoj6cph2bs)
__
---

[ADD YOUR NAME](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z1ebfvohm9lo82arslb4h04h4b227cbr8m9ibkjo_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cdgeaoj6cph2bs)
---

I’ve taken on mobsters. I’ve faced terrorists. I’ve battled corruption. I’ve been through it all. _Or so I thought..._
---
Never did I imagine that I would be the centerpiece of one of the Democrats’ most vindictive plots in history.
---
This entire plan by the Deep State to destroy a close friend of the President’s is truly the type of sinister scheme you’d think would only exist in the movies.
---
_But believe me, it exists; I’m living it! And if I fail to win this trial and defeat these sham charges, the Democrats will be able to jail me for the rest of my years on God’s green Earth._
---
These Deep State thugs know I have no pardon coming. No protection. No cavalry coming over the hill...
---
_...But what they continue to underestimate time and again is the collective strength of millions of faithful patriots like you who also _REFUSE_ to surrender to the Left’s tyranny. _
---
They entered my mugshot under booking #2313757, so my goal is for 2,313,757 patriots to sign my official petition — [**“THE PEOPLE’S PARDON OF RUDY GIULIANI**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zlrv8pgsjr145get9tooahfpn8idoei8qbjma6b8_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cdgeaoj6cph2bs)” — calling for my freedom and to DROP ALL CHARGES.
---
_At this final hour, can I count on you, Friend?_
---
[**Please help me reach my life-saving goal (_truly!_) today by immediately adding your name to “THE PEOPLE’S PARDON OF RUDY GIULIANI” as citizens unite to call for my freedom and to DROP ALL CHARGES against me.**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zqtt1f3nvruh5sov7quq963jgajb4943e1s1g0a8_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cdgeaoj6cph2bs)
---

[ADD YOUR NAME](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zcq25dg1a7ag5d4fgrfsj7b6ckp5a1nejctp8a48_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cdgeaoj6cph2bs)
---

Thank you and God bless you,
---
[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zhu5hh6e8jmlh8lg57u2d43cn877l8v78593csq8_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cdgeaoj6cph2bs)

body:
My fate is in your hands...

| [](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zi1fpkk3l8b6t62c9jc06uckkofh0p2fl96jrjqg_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cdge6p36c3h2bs)
---
Dear Friend,
---
The Radical Democrats think they’ve finally got me.
---
**You see, a Democrat Attorney General is making me stand in a rigged trial for bogus state-level crimes, because she knows that not even the most powerful man on the face of the planet – President Trump – can save me with a _federal_ pardon in _state_ court.**
---
_I’m left with no escape and no lifeline, especially after they’ve already STRIPPED me of my law licenses!_
---
But even though the most powerful man in the country legally cannot step in to save me from being **JAILED** for the rest of my life, the most powerful force absolutely can: _THE AMERICAN PEOPLE_.
---
With my back against the wall, I’m turning to the _ONE_ _THING_ that can save me: _**the collective voices of MILLIONS of patriots like YOU.**_

body:
Sometimes local leaders turn their focus away from...| | |
---
| | [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111573744/557680476/1701071684?refcode=EM_F_250716abbott_N_CD_90DO_9_G_N_Logo&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjAxYWY2MjBiLTQ5NjItZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=j_OlMIqUZfPic8zsYcCDeO1IPakZRsAL-0FrDB_X08I=)
---
Hi Peter,

Local government officials make key decisions that impact everything from the roads we drive on to the schools in our neighborhoods. Unfortunately, sometimes local leaders turn their focus away from local issues to push political agendas that hurt our communities.

That is what happened in a Pennsylvania town where the Board of Supervisors was becoming more and more extreme – and even cut funding to their local library after an LGBTQ+ event.

**That is why we want to introduce you to Carly Abbott, who is running for East Hempfield Township Supervisor in the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania.** She is running against MAGA Republican opponents including a “Moms for Liberty” candidate (_Moms for Liberty is an org that advocates against any school curriculum that mentions LGBTQ rights, race and ethnicity, critical race theory, and discrimination_).

[**Carly is committed to serving as a voice for the women of her township among a board of all men. Rush a $20.25 or more donation to help us support her campaign immediately →**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111573745/557680477/-2037633804?refcode=EM_F_250716abbott_N_CD_90DO_9_G_N_L1&amount=20.25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjAxYWY2MjBiLTQ5NjItZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=j_OlMIqUZfPic8zsYcCDeO1IPakZRsAL-0FrDB_X08I=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [**$20.25 »**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111573746/557680478/-2037633804?refcode=EM_F_250716abbott_N_CD_90DO_9_G_N_EB20.25&express_lane=true&amount=20.25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjAxYWY2MjBiLTQ5NjItZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=j_OlMIqUZfPic8zsYcCDeO1IPakZRsAL-0FrDB_X08I=)

Despite significant pushback from community members, the current **all male** board has drafted and enacted a resolution on immigration enforcement that was both hurtful and extreme – to end the town’s sanctuary status.
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111573752/557680484/-2037633804?refcode=EM_F_250716abbott_N_CD_90DO_9_G_N_P1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjAxYWY2MjBiLTQ5NjItZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=j_OlMIqUZfPic8zsYcCDeO1IPakZRsAL-0FrDB_X08I=)

Local government is not the space for extremism or political overreach. It’s the place for responsible and logical decisions to help the people of its community.

Carly is a perfect example of our work to uplift progressive, compassionate women’s voices. At Her Bold Move, we’re chipping away at male supermajorities across America; working to ensure there are no more rooms where decisions are being made exclusively by men.

Here’s what Carly had to say: “I will be a voice for the women of my township among a board of men. If I win, we will flip the board for the first time in history. When issues pertinent to women arise, we will finally have someone there to consider our interests.”

[**Carly needs our help to win in this critical local election. Help us rush support to her campaign by donating $20.25 or more today →**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111573753/557680485/-2037633804?refcode=EM_F_250716abbott_N_CD_90DO_9_G_N_L2&amount=20.25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjAxYWY2MjBiLTQ5NjItZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=j_OlMIqUZfPic8zsYcCDeO1IPakZRsAL-0FrDB_X08I=)

For women across America,

Colleen Davis
Endorsements Manager
Her Bold Move

subject: Is it time to get dark money out of our elections?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blue Amp Action PAC
body:

| Peter, with the disastrous Citizens United ruling, the Supreme Court let dark money dominate our elections. Since then, we’ve seen shady dark money groups and extremist billionaires use their wealth to usher in the MAGA movement!! Take right-wing weirdo Elon Musk. He spent HUNDREDS OF MILLIONS getting Trump reelected and making sure Trump’s cronies had control of our Congress. **But Democrats are standing up to big money! Some top Democrats are even working on amending our Constitution to limit dark money and put the power over our elections back in the hands of the people, not wealthy special interests!** [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T46f5ce5c-3d35-4a51-a61d-20b63fc5d106/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) **But we need to know if the American people actually back laws to end _Citizens United_ and stop radical special interest groups from using cash to control our elections!** [**Will you please take our survey by 11:59 PM tonight to let us know if you back Democrats in ending _Citizens United_?**](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tb87c5917-d92a-4ea6-b4f5-1b9fcbfc9b4b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| Thank you for your response! \- Blue Amp Action

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T1c9143ed-dddd-45ce-ab92-158f53d93120/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: Matt Gaetz
subject: Reading this email is the most important thing you can do today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by 1776 Project PAC PO Box 2891 Springfield, VA 22152 Not Authorized by any candidate or campaign committee
body:
I took a long time to gather my thoughts while writing this email. I NEED this email to count for something.
| [View in Web Browser](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e11923747/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3YvOG1jZ0U2c0dadg/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTA3MTY/LbmNsZEFCdTRrVw/qP2hpZHM9bmNZbHFTbXd5VG5LNERscw/gaHer0A/s1r964b3e94)
| | [](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e11923747/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUud2lucmVkLmNvbS8xNzc2LXByb2plY3QtcGFjL2dhZXR6c2hvcnQtcmVhZGluZ3RoaXMtZW0tb20tMi8/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTA3MTY/LbmNsZEFCdTRrVw/qP3V0bV9tZWRpdW09YnImcmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW10cG9zaXRpb249MSZ1dG1fdGVybT0yMDk4OV93cjE/gaHer0A/s3tb63bcaaf)
---
**Patriot, this is Matt Gaetz.**[**
**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e11923747/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUud2lucmVkLmNvbS8xNzc2LXByb2plY3QtcGFjL2dhZXR6c2hvcnQtcmVhZGluZ3RoaXMtZW0tb20tMi8/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTA3MTY/LbmNsZEFCdTRrVw/qP3V0bV9tZWRpdW09YnImcmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW10cG9zaXRpb249MSZ1dG1fdGVybT0yMDk4OV93cjE/gaHer0A/s3tb63bcaaf) _I took a long time to gather my thoughts while writing this email. I NEED this email to count for something._ So please, please read this all the way to the end. I've never asked the people who trust my word to support a Political Action Committee before because **I don't trust any of them.** **But this one's different.** I’m reaching out to you today to ask for you to support my friend Ryan Girdusky, founder of the 1776 Project PAC. Patriot, the 1776 Project PAC is changing the future of America, one small step at a time. [**As it stands, the radical left has a complete stranglehold of America's education system.**](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e11923747/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUud2lucmVkLmNvbS8xNzc2LXByb2plY3QtcGFjL2dhZXR6c2hvcnQtcmVhZGluZ3RoaXMtZW0tb20tMi8/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTA3MTY/LbmNsZEFCdTRrVw/qP3V0bV9tZWRpdW09YnImcmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW10cG9zaXRpb249MSZ1dG1fdGVybT0yMDk4OV93cjE/gaHer0A/s3tb63bcaaf) This didn't happen by accident. Rather, the left’s control of our education system is a result of a decades-long, coordinated attack from wealthy leftist donors and organizations. The results of this attack have been **DEVASTATING.** 79% of all political donations made by teachers (the people who spend 8 hours a day “educating” our children) go to DEMOCRATS... **Woke gender ideology** and **America-hating propaganda** has made its way to every school in the country...
